Handler structs may carry unexported zero-value state; only unexported
fields whose type has a provider error.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import (
	"net/http"
	"sync"
)

type Greeter struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewGreeter() *Greeter { return &Greeter{} }

type Handlers struct {
	Greeter *Greeter
	mu      sync.Mutex
	count   int
}

//fabrik:http GET /
func (h *Handlers) Index(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
	h.mu.Lock()
	h.count++
	h.mu.Unlock()
}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"

	"app/web"
)

func run() int {
	if err := func() error {
		// Providers
		webGreeter := web.NewGreeter()
		webHandlers := &web.Handlers{
			Greeter: webGreeter,
		}

		r := router.New()

		// Register
		r.Method("GET", "/", webHandlers.Index)
		return nil
	}(); err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "app:", err)
		return 1
	}
	return 0
}
